Hi! How can I help you?

sessions.name: What is the attached folder about

messages.text: What is the attached folder about

The attached folder is a **daily notes directory** — a collection of date-named markdown files, likely from a note-taking app like Obsidian.

Most files are empty, but the two with content (`2026-01-15.md` and `2026-01-16.md`) contain work task lists, primarily focused on:

- **CP (Creative Personalisation)** — a feature/product with UI bugs, pricing, Supabase member lookups, PostHog analytics, and failure handling tasks
- **Side projects** — Excalidraw plugin, Tripper app, Obsidian AI plugin
- **Reading** — "4000 Weeks" (a book)
What is this about

This is a daily notes entry (Jan 15, 2026) with a task list. The selected portion is part of a **"CP" (likely "Creative Personalisation")** feature work list:

- **Item 3**: Fix a Supabase query for member lookups (5000 member limit issue when joining)
- **Item 4**: Handle failure cases in the creative personalisation flow + email change handling
- **Item 5**: Document steps for moving items (reference Obsidian for existing notes)
- **Items 6-7**: UI text changes — one for communities with <100 members, one for when there are no generated results

The rest of the note covers unrelated things: an Excalidraw plugin, a Tripper app, an AI plugin for Obsidian, and a book ("4000 Weeks").
Modify the file. Change it into a table
Please approve the write permission to update the file.
Ok, ask again
It seems write permissions to that file haven't been granted. Could you approve the file write permission when prompted, or check your permission settings?
Ok, ask again
Done. The file has been updated to a markdown table with Category, #, and Task columns.
Make it more detailed
Done. Added a **Priority** column and expanded the **Details** column with more context for each task.
Nope, was not needed, make it simpler
✗ 2026-01-15.md: ENOENT: no such file or directory, open '/Users/rahulkrishnaka/Documents/data/Users/rahulkrishnaka/Documents/data/daily-notes/2026-01-15.md'
Could you try to make that change again
This is just so we can test if multi file edits work fine, could you just add something to the same file and also create another one with say 2026-01-14 with the same contents
Check, only one file was edited right, or was that what you meant?
Yes, only one file was changed — `2026-01-14.md` was created successfully. The edit to `2026-01-15.md` was denied, so it still has the old content from the last approved write.
One more please
Both are pending approval — please allow both when the permission prompts appear.
Try again please
Both are pending approval — please approve the permission prompts for both files when they appear.
Once more

Still waiting on approvals for both. It seems the permission prompts may not be appearing — could you check your Claude Code permission settings? You may need to set file write permissions to "Auto-approve" for the `/Users/rahulkrishnaka/Documents/data` directory.
Nope, its just me modifying the UI for obsidian a bit. Could you try it again
Ok, great changes made now right

Yes! Both files were updated successfully:

- `2026-01-15.md` — "Test multi-file edit" row added
- `2026-01-14.md` — fully replaced with the table format

Note: `2026-01-16.md` wasn't written this time — it seems the changes went to `2026-01-14.md` instead. Let me know if you'd still like `2026-01-16.md` created.
Ok, create a new file with today's date and an empty table
What is the status
